response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack software engineer with 15+ years of experience and a CS degree from MIT, satisfying the raw experience bar, but his background is almost entirely web, mobile, and backend development. He shows no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator or GPU architecture work, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design — the core competencies of this principal-level performance architect role. His low-latency DLang systems work is the closest adjacency, but it does not bridge the gap to silicon-level performance modeling.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded work
C/C++ systems experience at National Instruments plus sustained open-source contributions
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning, GPU, or AI accelerator workload background (all preferred and required skill areas)
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture at principal level

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning or GPU workload expertise
Low-latency systems and performance-oriented backend work
